payload.text:
# Google AI Studio Prompt: Create "Free Spinal MRI Review" Page ## OBJECTIVE Create a dedicated landing page for Dr. Jonathan J. Rasouli's **Free Spinal MRI Review** service. This page should convert visitors into consultation bookings by clearly explaining the value of the complimentary MRI review, what's included, and making it easy to submit their imaging for review. --- ## PAGE SPECIFICATIONS ### Page URL Structure **URL:** `/free-mri-review` or `/free-spinal-mri-review` **Page Title (SEO):** "Free Spinal MRI Review | Dr. Jonathan Rasouli | NYC & Staten Island Neurosurgeon" **Meta Description:** "Get a free expert review of your spinal MRI from board-certified neurosurgeon Dr. Jonathan Rasouli. Understand your diagnosis and explore treatment options. No obligation consultation." --- ## NAVIGATION INTEGRATION ### Header Navigation Update Add **two prominent CTA buttons** to the main navigation on ALL pages: **Button 1: Schedule Consultation (Blue - Existing)** - Background Color: #0066CC (Blue) - Text: White - Text: "Schedule Consultation" - Link: `/contact` or appointment form **Button 2: Free MRI Review (Orange - NEW)** - Background Color: #FF6B35 (Vibrant Orange) - Text: White - Text: "Free MRI Review" - Link: `/free-mri-review` - Icon: 🔍 or 📋 (optional) **HTML Structure for Navigation Buttons:** ```html
Schedule Consultation 🔍 Free MRI Review
``` **CSS Styling:** ```css .header-cta-buttons { display: flex; gap: 12px; align-items: center; } .cta-btn { padding: 12px 24px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 15px; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; transition: all 0.3s ease; white-space: nowrap; } .primary-cta { background: #0066CC; color: white; border: 2px solid #0066CC; } .primary-cta:hover { background: #0052A3; border-color: #0052A3; transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(0,102,204,0.3); } .orange-cta { background: #FF6B35; color: white; border: 2px solid #FF6B35; } .orange-cta:hover { background: #E55A2B; border-color: #E55A2B; transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(255,107,53,0.3); } /* Mobile responsive */ @media (max-width: 768px) { .header-cta-buttons { flex-direction: column; width: 100%; gap: 8px; } .cta-btn { width: 100%; text-align: center; padding: 14px 20px; } } ``` --- ## PAGE CONTENT STRUCTURE ### Hero Section **Background:** Light medical blue gradient or high-quality spine MRI image (blurred/overlayed) **Hero Content (Centered):** ``` 🔍 Free Spinal MRI Review GET EXPERT ANALYSIS FROM A BOARD-CERTIFIED NEUROSURGEON [Headline - H1] Not Sure What Your Spine MRI Results Mean? Get a Second Opinion From Dr. Rasouli – Absolutely Free [Subheadline] Board-certified, dual fellowship-trained neurosurgeon offering complimentary expert review of your spinal imaging with personalized treatment recommendations [Two CTA Buttons Side by Side] [Orange Button] Submit Your MRI for Review [White Outline Button] Call (718) 226-4940 [Trust Indicators Below] ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ 5.0 Patient Rating | ✓ No Obligation | ✓ Confidential | ✓ 24-48 Hour Review ``` --- ### What's Included Section **Section Title:** "What You'll Receive with Your Free MRI Review" **Visual:** 3-column grid (stacks on mobile) with icons **Column 1:** **Icon:** 👨‍⚕️ (Doctor icon) **Title:** Expert Analysis **Description:** "Dr. Rasouli personally reviews your MRI imaging to identify any spinal conditions, nerve compression, disc problems, or structural abnormalities that may be causing your symptoms." **Column 2:** **Icon:** 📋 (Document/Report icon) **Title:** Written Report **Description:** "Receive a detailed, easy-to-understand explanation of your imaging findings in plain language, not medical jargon. You'll know exactly what's happening with your spine." **Column 3:** **Icon:** 💡 (Lightbulb/Idea icon) **Title:** Treatment Options **Description:** "Get personalized recommendations for your specific condition, from conservative care to advanced surgical options. Understand all your choices with no pressure." **Below Columns - Additional Benefits (Checklist Style):** ✅ **No Cost** - Completely free with no hidden fees or obligations ✅ **Quick Turnaround** - Most reviews completed within 24-48 hours ✅ **Second Opinion** - Valuable even if you've already seen another doctor ✅ **Confidential** - HIPAA-compliant secure review process ✅ **No Commitment** - No obligation to become a patient ✅ **Virtual Review** - Submit from anywhere, no office visit required --- ### Why Get an MRI Review Section **Section Title:** "Why Should You Get Your Spinal MRI Reviewed?" **Content (2-column layout on desktop, single column mobile):** **Left Column - Reasons:** **When to Request a Free MRI Review:** 📌 **You've had an MRI but don't understand the results** Many radiology reports are filled with medical terminology that's confusing for patients. Dr. Rasouli translates these findings into clear, actionable information. 📌 **Your symptoms don't match what you were told** Sometimes there's a disconnect between what the MRI shows and what you're experiencing. A second expert opinion can reveal overlooked findings. 📌 **You want to know all your treatment options** Different spine specialists have different approaches. Dr. Rasouli's dual fellowship training gives him unique perspective on both surgical and non-surgical solutions. 📌 **You're considering surgery and want a second opinion** Spine surgery is a major decision. Getting confirmation from another board-certified neurosurgeon provides peace of mind. 📌 **Conservative treatment hasn't worked** If physical therapy, injections, or medications haven't helped, an MRI review can identify whether there's a structural problem that needs different treatment. 📌 **You have new or worsening symptoms** If your back or neck pain has changed, or you're experiencing numbness, weakness, or tingling, a fresh review of your imaging can identify progression. **Right Column - Dr. Rasouli's Expertise:** **Why Trust Dr. Rasouli's Review?** [Professional Headshot of Dr. Rasouli] ✓ **Board-Certified Neurosurgeon** American Board of Neurological Surgery ✓ **Dual Fellowship-Trained** Rare combination of neurosurgery AND orthopaedic spine surgery expertise ✓ **Former Cleveland Clinic Faculty** Trained at world-renowned spine surgery program ✓ **Complex Spine Specialist** Focuses on challenging cases other surgeons may not handle ✓ **Published Researcher** Actively contributes to spine surgery literature ✓ **Perfect Patient Ratings** 5.0 stars across all review platforms ✓ **Minimally Invasive Expert** Specializes in advanced robotic and minimally invasive techniques [Orange Button] Submit Your MRI Now --- ### What to Expect Section **Section Title:** "How the Free MRI Review Process Works" **Timeline/Process Visual (Horizontal steps on desktop, vertical on mobile):** **Step 1: Submit Your Imaging** **Icon:** 📤 Upload "Use our secure form to upload your MRI images (CD or digital files) along with brief information about your symptoms and medical history. Don't have digital files? We can help." ↓ **Step 2: Expert Review** **Icon:** 🔬 Analysis "Dr. Rasouli personally reviews your spinal MRI, analyzing every detail to identify conditions affecting your spine and nerves. This typically takes 24-48 hours." ↓ **Step 3: Receive Your Report** **Icon:** 📧 Results "You'll receive a detailed written report via secure email explaining your MRI findings in understandable language, along with Dr. Rasouli's assessment." ↓ **Step 4: Discuss Your Options** **Icon:** 📞 Consultation "If you'd like to discuss your results in detail, we'll schedule a complimentary phone or video consultation to answer all your questions and explain treatment options." **Important Note:** "While the MRI review is free, if you decide to schedule an in-person consultation or treatment, standard consultation fees and insurance policies apply." --- ### Conditions We Review Section **Section Title:** "Spinal Conditions Dr. Rasouli Identifies in MRI Reviews" **Grid Layout (3 columns desktop, 1 column mobile):** **Common Findings:** - ✓ Herniated discs (cervical, thoracic, lumbar) - ✓ Spinal stenosis (narrowing of spinal canal) - ✓ Degenerative disc disease - ✓ Bulging discs - ✓ Nerve root compression - ✓ Spinal cord compression (myelopathy) - ✓ Spondylolisthesis (vertebral slippage) - ✓ Spinal instability - ✓ Facet joint arthritis - ✓ Spinal deformities (scoliosis, kyphosis) - ✓ Foraminal stenosis (pinched nerves) - ✓ Spinal tumors or masses - ✓ Post-surgical changes - ✓ Failed back surgery syndrome - ✓ Vertebral compression fractures [Orange Button] Get Your MRI Reviewed --- ### Submission Form Section **Section Title:** "Submit Your MRI for Free Expert Review" **Form Design:** Clean, professional, HIPAA-compliant **Form Introduction:** "Complete the form below to submit your spinal MRI for Dr. Rasouli's review. All information is transmitted securely and kept strictly confidential under HIPAA regulations." **Form Fields:** **Personal Information:** ``` [Full Name]* (required) First Name: ________________ Last Name: ________________ [Email Address]* (required) Email: ____________________________________ [Phone Number]* (required) Phone: ____________________________________ Preferred Contact Method: ☐ Phone ☐ Email ☐ Either [Date of Birth]* DOB: ____ / ____ / ________ ``` **Imaging Information:** ``` [MRI Date]* When was your MRI taken? ____ / ____ / ________ [Body Region]* ☐ Cervical Spine (Neck) ☐ Thoracic Spine (Mid-back) ☐ Lumbar Spine (Lower back) ☐ Multiple regions [MRI Facility] Where was your MRI performed? ____________________ ``` **Symptoms & History:** ``` [Current Symptoms]* (required) Please describe your symptoms (pain location, numbness, weakness, etc.): [Text area - 5 rows] [Symptom Duration]* How long have you had these symptoms? ☐ Less than 1 month ☐ 1-3 months ☐ 3-6 months ☐ 6-12 months ☐ More than 1 year [Previous Treatment] What treatments have you tried? (Check all that apply) ☐ Physical therapy ☐ Chiropractic care ☐ Pain medications ☐ Muscle relaxers ☐ Epidural steroid injections ☐ Nerve blocks ☐ Surgery (please specify): ________________ ☐ Other: ________________ [Current Diagnosis] (if known) Has another doctor given you a diagnosis? [Text area - 2 rows] ``` **MRI Upload:** ``` [Upload MRI Files]* **How to Submit Your MRI:** Option 1: Digital Upload ☐ I have digital files (DICOM, CD images, or PDF report) [Upload Button] Choose Files (Max 500MB total) Accepted formats: .dcm, .zip, .pdf, .jpg, .png Option 2: Request Transfer ☐ I need help obtaining my MRI images from the imaging center Facility Name: ____________________ Facility Phone: ____________________ We'll contact them on your behalf to request your images. Option 3: Mail CD ☐ I will mail my MRI CD to your office Mailing Address: Dr. Jonathan Rasouli 501 Seaview Avenue, Suite 201 Staten Island, NY 10305 ``` **Insurance & Preferences:** ``` [Insurance Information] (optional) Do you have health insurance? ☐ Yes ☐ No Insurance Carrier: ____________________ (Note: The MRI review is free regardless of insurance status) [Preferred Office Location] (for future appointments) ☐ Staten Island Office ☐ Manhattan Office ☐ No preference [How Did You Hear About Us?] ☐ Google search ☐ Social media ☐ Referral from doctor ☐ Friend/family recommendation ☐ Patient review site (Healthgrades, Vitals, etc.) ☐ Other: ________________ ``` **Consent & Privacy:** ``` [Checkbox]* I acknowledge that I have read and agree to the Privacy Policy and Terms of Service [Checkbox]* I authorize Dr. Rasouli to review my medical imaging and provide an assessment [Checkbox] I would like to receive email updates about spine health and treatment options from Dr. Rasouli's practice (optional) **Privacy Notice:** Your information is protected under HIPAA regulations and will be kept strictly confidential. By submitting this form, you consent to Dr. Rasouli's review of your imaging for the purpose of providing a medical opinion. ``` **Submit Button (Large, Prominent, Orange):** ```html ``` **Below Submit Button:** ``` 🔒 Secure Transmission | ✓ HIPAA Compliant | ⏱️ 24-48 Hour Review Time Questions about submitting your MRI? Call us at (718) 226-4940 ``` --- ### FAQ Section **Section Title:** "Frequently Asked Questions About MRI Reviews" **Accordion-style FAQ (expandable/collapsible):** **Q: Is the MRI review really free?** A: Yes, absolutely. Dr. Rasouli offers complimentary MRI reviews as a service to help patients understand their spine conditions. There is no cost and no obligation to become a patient. **Q: How long does the review take?** A: Most MRI reviews are completed within 24-48 hours. You'll receive an email when your report is ready. Complex cases may take slightly longer. **Q: What if I don't have digital copies of my MRI?** A: No problem. You can request your MRI images from the facility where you had the scan. Most imaging centers provide images on CD or can send them electronically. We can also help you request them if needed. **Q: Will I need to schedule an office visit?** A: Not for the free MRI review. Dr. Rasouli reviews your imaging remotely and provides a written report. If you'd like to discuss results in detail or pursue treatment, we can schedule a phone, video, or in-person consultation. **Q: What happens after I receive my report?** A: You'll have several options: discuss results via phone/video consultation (no charge), schedule an in-person consultation (standard fees apply), use the information for your own records, or seek treatment with your current doctor. It's entirely up to you. **Q: Can I get a review if I live outside New York?** A: Yes! The MRI review process is entirely remote, so we can review imaging from anywhere. However, if you need in-person treatment, you would need to travel to our NYC or Staten Island office. **Q: How detailed is the report?** A: Very detailed. Dr. Rasouli provides a comprehensive analysis in easy-to-understand language, explaining what was found on your MRI, what it means clinically, and what treatment options are appropriate for your condition. **Q: Is my information kept confidential?** A: Absolutely. All submissions are HIPAA-compliant and your personal information and medical imaging are kept strictly confidential. We use secure, encrypted transmission methods. **Q: What if another doctor already reviewed my MRI?** A: That's fine! Second opinions are extremely valuable in spine care. Dr. Rasouli's dual fellowship training and expertise in complex spine conditions often reveals additional insights or alternative treatment approaches. **Q: Will you recommend surgery?** A: Only if it's truly necessary. Dr. Rasouli believes in a conservative-first approach and will always discuss non-surgical options. If surgery is recommended, he'll explain why and what alternatives exist. **Q: Does insurance cover this?** A: The initial MRI review is free and doesn't involve insurance. If you later decide to schedule an in-person consultation or treatment, standard insurance policies apply and we accept most major plans. **Q: What file formats do you accept?** A: We accept DICOM files (standard MRI format), CD images, ZIP files containing imaging, and PDF radiology reports. If you have your MRI on CD, you can mail it to us or we can help you convert it to digital format. **Q: Can I submit MRIs of multiple body regions?** A: Yes, you can submit imaging of your cervical (neck), thoracic (mid-back), and lumbar (lower back) spine. Please indicate all regions when submitting. **Q: What if my MRI is very old?** A: Dr. Rasouli can review MRIs of any age, but if your imaging is more than 6-12 months old and your symptoms have changed, we may recommend updated imaging for the most accurate assessment. [Orange Button] Submit Your MRI Now --- ### Testimonials Section **Section Title:** "What Patients Say About Dr. Rasouli's Expertise" **3 Testimonial Cards (Carousel on mobile):** **Testimonial 1:** ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Dr. Rasouli took the time to thoroughly review my MRI and explain everything in terms I could understand. After months of not knowing what was wrong, I finally had clarity and a path forward. His analysis was spot-on and his recommendations helped me avoid unnecessary surgery." — Michael D. | Staten Island, NY **Testimonial 2:** ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I had been told by another surgeon that I needed major spinal fusion. Dr. Rasouli's review of my MRI revealed a less invasive option that worked perfectly. I'm so grateful for his second opinion and expertise." — Sarah M. | Manhattan, NY **Testimonial 3:** ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "The detailed report I received from Dr. Rasouli was incredibly helpful. He identified issues my previous doctor missed and gave me realistic expectations about treatment. His honesty and expertise are unmatched." — Robert L. | Brooklyn, NY --- ### Trust & Credentials Section **Section Title:** "Why Patients Trust Dr. Rasouli's MRI Analysis" **Grid of Credential Badges/Icons:** ✓ **Board-Certified Neurosurgeon** American Board of Neurological Surgery ✓ **Alpha Omega Alpha Graduate** Top 10% of medical school class ✓ **Dual Fellowship-Trained** Neurosurgery + Orthopaedic Spine Surgery ✓ **Cleveland Clinic Faculty** Former Assistant Professor ✓ **Published Expert** Numerous peer-reviewed articles ✓ **Media-Recognized** Featured in CNN, Washington Post ✓ **5.0 Star Rating** Perfect patient satisfaction ✓ **Complex Spine Specialist** Advanced surgical expertise [Orange Button] Get Your Free MRI Review --- ### Final CTA Section **Background:** Dark blue gradient with subtle spine illustration **Content (Centered, White Text):** ``` Ready to Understand Your Spine MRI Results? Get Expert Analysis from Dr. Rasouli – Completely Free [Large Orange Button] Submit Your MRI Now [White Outline Button] Call (718) 226-4940 to Discuss Available in Staten Island & Manhattan Most reviews completed in 24-48 hours ``` --- ## TECHNICAL SPECIFICATIONS ### Page Performance Requirements - Load time: <2.5 seconds - Mobile-optimized form (easy to complete on phone) - File upload functionality (support up to 500MB) - Progress indicator for upload - Form validation with helpful error messages - Success confirmation page after submission ### SEO Optimization **Primary Keywords:** - free spinal MRI review - second opinion spine MRI - neurosurgeon MRI review NYC - free spine MRI analysis - spinal MRI interpretation **Secondary Keywords:** - spine MRI results explained - back pain MRI review - herniated disc MRI second opinion - spinal stenosis MRI analysis **Schema Markup:** ```json { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "MedicalWebPage", "name": "Free Spinal MRI Review", "description": "Free expert spinal MRI review by board-certified neurosurgeon Dr. Jonathan Rasouli", "specialty": "Neurosurgery", "medicalAudience": "Patient" } ``` ### Form Functionality **Backend Requirements:** - Secure file upload (encrypted transmission) - Email notification to office when form submitted - Auto-responder email to patient confirming receipt - HIPAA-compliant data storage - Virus scanning on uploaded files - File type validation - Size limit enforcement **Success Page:** After form submission, redirect to confirmation page: ``` ✅ Your MRI Submission Has Been Received Thank you for submitting your spinal MRI for review! What Happens Next: 1. You'll receive a confirmation email within a few minutes 2. Dr. Rasouli will personally review your imaging within 24-48 hours 3. You'll receive a detailed report via secure email 4. Our office will contact you to discuss scheduling a consultation (optional) Confirmation Number: [UNIQUE_ID] Questions? Call us at (718) 226-4940 [Button] Return to Home Page ``` ### Analytics Tracking **Track These Events:** - Page views on MRI review page - Form starts (user begins filling out form) - Form completions (successful submissions) - File uploads - Button clicks (all CTA buttons) - FAQ expansions (which questions viewed most) - Exit points (where users leave page) **Conversion Goal:** Form submission = primary conversion --- ## DESIGN SPECIFICATIONS ### Color Palette for This Page - **Primary Orange:** #FF6B35 (CTA buttons, accents) - **Secondary Blue:** #0066CC (links, secondary buttons) - **Dark Blue:** #1E4D8B (headings, trust elements) - **White:** #FFFFFF (backgrounds, cards) - **Light Gray:** #F5F5F5 (section backgrounds) - **Text:** #333333 (body text) - **Success Green:** #28A745 (confirmation messages) ### Typography - **Page H1:** 44px, bold, color #1E4D8B - **Section H2:** 36px, semi-bold, color #1E4D8B - **H3 Subheadings:** 24px, medium, color #333333 - **Body Text:** 17px, regular, color #333333, line-height 1.7 - **Button Text:** 17px, bold, uppercase for main CTAs ### Button Specifications **Primary Orange Button (Main CTA):** ```css .orange-cta-main { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #FF6B35 0%, #FF8C42 100%); color: white; padding: 18px 40px; font-size: 18px; font-weight: 700; border: none; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 4px 15px rgba(255,107,53,0.3); cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.3s ease; } .orange-cta-main:hover { transform: translateY(-3px); box-shadow: 0 6px 20px rgba(255,107,53,0.4); } ``` **Secondary Button (White Outline):** ```css .white-outline-btn { background: transparent; color: white; border: 2px solid white; padding: 16px 32px; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; border-radius: 8px; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.3s ease; } .white-outline-btn:hover { background: white; color: #FF6B35; } ``` ### Icons to Use - 🔍 (Magnifying glass) - MRI review/analysis - 📋 (Clipboard) - Report/documentation - 💡 (Lightbulb) - Insights/recommendations - 👨‍⚕️ (Doctor) - Expert analysis - 📤 (Upload) - Submit imaging - 📧 (Email) - Receive report - 📞 (Phone) - Consultation - ✓ (Checkmark) - Benefits/features - ⭐ (Star) - Ratings/testimonials - 🔒 (Lock) - Security/privacy ### Mobile Responsiveness - Form fields stack vertically on mobile - Buttons full-width on mobile (<768px) - Upload button prominent and easy to tap - Process steps display vertically on mobile - FAQs expand/collapse for space efficiency - Sticky "Submit MRI" button on mobile scroll --- ## INTEGRATION WITH EXISTING SITE ### Navigation Menu Add "Free MRI Review" to main navigation: ``` Home | About Dr. Rasouli | Services | Patient Resources | Free MRI Review | Contact ``` ### Footer Add link under "Patient Resources" section: ``` Patient Resources: - New Patients - Insurance & Billing - Patient Forms - Free MRI Review ← NEW - FAQs ``` ### Homepage Integration Add promotional banner or section on homepage: **"Not Sure What Your MRI Results Mean?"** Get a free expert review from Dr. Rasouli [Orange Button] Learn More About Free MRI Review ### Pop-Up/Slide-In (Optional) Exit-intent popup: ``` Wait! Before You Go... Get Your Spinal MRI Reviewed by Dr. Rasouli – FREE ✓ Expert neurosurgeon analysis ✓ 24-48 hour turnaround ✓ No obligation or cost [Orange Button] Submit Your MRI [Link] No thanks, I'll browse more ``` --- ## LEGAL & COMPLIANCE ### Required Disclaimers **On Page:** "This free MRI review service provides educational information and medical opinion based on imaging analysis. It does not establish a doctor-patient relationship. The review is not a substitute for an in-person medical examination. Results and recommendations are based solely on submitted imaging and information provided." **Privacy Statement:** "All submissions are HIPAA-compliant. Your personal information and medical imaging are encrypted during transmission and stored securely. We will not share your information with third parties without your explicit consent." **Terms of Service Note:** "By submitting your MRI for review, you agree to our Terms of Service and Privacy Policy. You authorize Dr. Rasouli to review your medical imaging for the sole purpose of providing a medical opinion." --- ## SUCCESS METRICS **Primary KPIs:** - Form submission rate (target: 10-15% of page visitors) - Form completion rate (started vs. finished) - Time on page (target: 3+ minutes) - Bounce rate (target: <40%) - Conversions to scheduled consultations **Secondary KPIs:** - Traffic to page from homepage - Mobile vs. desktop submission rates - File upload success rate - Email open rate for confirmation - Phone call conversions --- ## CONTENT TONE & MESSAGING **Key Messages to Convey:** 1. **Free & No Obligation** - Emphasized throughout 2. **Expert Credentials** - Dr. Rasouli's unique qualifications 3. **Easy Process** - Simple, quick, remote 4. **Confidential & Secure** - HIPAA-compliant 5. **Patient-Centered** - Focused on helping, not selling **Tone:** - Helpful and educational - Professional but approachable - Empathetic to patient concerns - Confident without being pushy - Clear and jargon-free **Avoid:** - High-pressure sales language - Medical jargon without explanation - Guarantees of specific outcomes - Comparison to other doctors - Fear-based messaging --- ## LAUNCH CHECKLIST **Before Going Live:** - [ ] Test form submission on all devices - [ ] Test file upload (various file types and sizes) - [ ] Verify email confirmations send correctly - [ ] Check all CTA button links - [ ] Mobile responsive design verified - [ ] Page speed under 2.5 seconds - [ ] SSL certificate active - [ ] Analytics tracking installed - [ ] Legal disclaimers present - [ ] Privacy policy updated - [ ] Staff trained on new submissions process - [ ] Email templates for responses ready **Post-Launch:** - [ ] Monitor form submissions daily - [ ] Track conversion rates weekly - [ ] A/B test CTA button text/colors - [ ] Gather patient feedback - [ ] Optimize based on analytics - [ ] Update FAQ based on questions received --- ## SUMMARY This "Free Spinal MRI Review" page is designed to: 1. **Generate leads** through a valuable free service 2. **Build trust** by demonstrating expertise before commitment 3. **Remove barriers** to initial consultation 4. **Differentiate** Dr. Rasouli from competitors 5. **Convert visitors** into patients The orange CTA button provides strong visual contrast with the existing blue "Schedule Consultation" button, giving users two clear paths: immediate appointment booking or free MRI review first. **Implementation Priority:** HIGH - This is a powerful lead generation and conversion tool that reduces patient anxiety about first contact and demonstrates Dr. Rasouli's expertise and confidence in his abilities.

**Formulating a Strategy**

I'm currently focused on the core objective: building a new landing page specifically for free spinal MRI reviews. I've broken down the request into key components, specifically outlining what needs to happen to make the /free-mri-review route possible. Next, I'll be focusing on the key features this new page requires.


**Detailing Page Components**

I'm now fully immersed in the nitty-gritty of the new landing page, breaking down the essential pieces. I'm prioritizing the structure, which includes the hero section, the 'what's included', the 'why get a review', the step-by-step process, relevant conditions, a complex submission form, FAQs, testimonials, and credentials, all within a mobile-responsive design, as specified. The color palette will center around medical blue and vibrant orange. I'm considering best practices to manage the form state, validation, and simulating file uploads, but keeping the state local is fine for a single page.


**Integrating the Review Route**

I'm now integrating the new route and components. First, I will update `constants.tsx` to include the route in `NAV_LINKS`, new testimonial data, and FAQs. Next, I will update `Layout.tsx` for the two-button strategy in the desktop navigation and incorporate the link in the mobile nav. Finally, I will create the large `MRIReviewForm.tsx` component, potentially keeping it within the page file.


**Developing the Form Component**

I'm now fully immersed in developing the MRI review form. Initially, I'll build the dedicated `MRIReviewForm` component within the `FreeMRIReview.tsx` page file, to streamline the process. The focus is on a comprehensive, multi-step or long-scroll form with file upload capabilities and a user-friendly UI. I am also currently working on the structure of the page itself and the page file, including the hero, 'what's included', 'why', process steps, conditions, FAQ, testimonials, and credentials sections. I have updated the `App.tsx` file with the `/free-mri-review` route.


**Modifying the UI**

I'm now revising the design and code structure based on feedback, specifically addressing navigation and form simulation. I need to update `Layout.tsx` to handle the two-button header requirement separate from the text links. I also need to simulate file uploads and progress within the form UI without actual backend file handling. Finally, I will add the provided orange color to the Tailwind config. I'm focusing on `index.html`, `constants.tsx`, `Layout.tsx`, and the `FreeMRIReview.tsx` components.


**Building the Page**

I'm now fully immersed in the construction of the Free MRI Review page, file by file. I'm focusing on key UI components, specifically `lucide-react` icons (Search, FileText, Upload), and form logic. I'm creating simple state for input fields and file selection. The work is organized by updating `index.html` to include the specific orange color, `constants.tsx` to include the new link, and `Layout.tsx` to handle the new buttons in both desktop and mobile navigation. The bulk of the work will be in the `FreeMRIReview.tsx` page file, following the content specifications. The form itself will look professional and use standard inputs with good spacing.


**Implementing Final Details**

Now, I'm working on the final details. I'll implement a drag-and-drop look for the file upload section. I'll use simple toggle state for the FAQ section. I'm checking the `Layout` logic to prevent link duplication, deciding to remove the 'Contact' link from `NAV_LINKS` in `constants` for mobile. For the desktop header, I'll manually separate CTAs to resolve duplication issues. I'll assemble the final code, ensuring mobile and desktop consistency. I am now proceeding to generate XML.


**Adjusting the Navigation**

I'm now revising the navigation structure. I'll include the "Free MRI Review" text link in the `NAV_LINKS`, probably for mobile. On desktop, I'll prioritize the two CTA buttons: "Schedule Consultation" and "Free MRI Review," potentially hiding the text link to avoid redundancy and prioritize the buttons. I'll need to modify `Layout.tsx` for this adjustment.


**Polishing the Imports**

I'm now making sure all imports are correct, especially the Lucide icons. I'm also addressing two self-corrections: updating the Tailwind config in `index.html` as the full content was provided, and structuring the large `FreeMRIReview.tsx` file for better readability. I'll need to modify the Mobile Menu/Footer, and add the text link there.

I will implement the "Free Spinal MRI Review" landing page and update the navigation to include the requested CTA buttons.

Here is the plan:
1.  **`index.html`**: Add the vibrant orange color (`#FF6B35`) to the Tailwind configuration.
2.  **`constants.tsx`**: Update `NAV_LINKS` to include the MRI review page for mobile/footer use.
3.  **`components/Layout.tsx`**: Redesign the header to feature the two specific CTA buttons (Blue for Consultation, Orange for MRI Review) and update the mobile menu.
4.  **`App.tsx`**: Add the new route.
5.  **`pages/FreeMRIReview.tsx`**: Create the comprehensive landing page with the secure submission form, FAQs, and detailed service information.

entries.description: Add vibrant orange color to Tailwind theme config
Add Free MRI Review to navigation links
Update desktop header with dual CTA buttons and mobile navigation

import React, { useState, useEffect } from 'react'; import { NavLink, Link, useLocation } from 'react-router-dom'; import { Menu, X, Phone, MapPin, Mail, Shield, Navigation, Search } from 'lucide-react'; import { NAV_LINKS, CONTACT_INFO } from '../constants'; import Button from './Button'; const Layout: React.FC<{ children: React.ReactNode }> = ({ children }) => { const [isMenuOpen, setIsMenuOpen] = useState(false); const location = useLocation(); // Scroll to top on route change useEffect(() => { window.scrollTo(0, 0); setIsMenuOpen(false); }, [location]); // Filter links for Desktop Navbar to avoid duplication with buttons const desktopLinks = NAV_LINKS.filter(link => link.path !== '/contact' && link.path !== '/free-mri-review' ); return (

payload.text:
# Google AI Studio Prompt: Create "Media & Press" Page for Dr. Jonathan J. Rasouli ## OBJECTIVE Create a comprehensive "Media & Press" page showcasing Dr. Jonathan J. Rasouli's media appearances, patient success stories, published research, speaking engagements, and recognition. This page establishes Dr. Rasouli as a thought leader and trusted medical expert while humanizing his practice through compelling patient stories. --- ## PAGE SPECIFICATIONS ### Page URL Structure **URL:** `/media` or `/press-and-media` or `/in-the-news` **Page Title (SEO):** "Media & Press | Dr. Jonathan Rasouli | Featured Neurosurgeon - NYC" **Meta Description:** "Dr. Jonathan Rasouli featured in CNN, The Washington Post, NY Post and more. Read inspiring patient stories and latest neurosurgery insights from a leading NYC spine surgeon." --- ## PAGE STRUCTURE ### Hero Section **Background:** Professional gradient (deep blue to lighter blue) or high-quality medical facility image **Hero Content:** ``` Dr. Rasouli in the Media [Headline - H1] Trusted Voice in Neurosurgery & Spine Care [Subheadline] Featured expert in major publications and media outlets, sharing insights on complex spine conditions, innovative surgical techniques, and inspiring patient recoveries [Media Logos Row - Grayscale/Color on hover] [CNN Logo] [NY Post Logo] [Washington Post Logo] [Becker's Spine Logo] ``` **Below Hero - Quick Stats Bar:** ``` 📺 Featured in National Media | 📰 15+ Publications | 🎤 International Speaker | ⭐ Patient Success Stories ``` --- ## MAIN CONTENT SECTIONS ### Section 1: Featured Media Appearances **Section Title:** "Dr. Rasouli in the News" **Subsection: Patient Success Stories** Create expandable/featured story cards with the following structure: --- #### **FEATURED STORY 1: Anthony Mercado - "Miracle Recovery"** **Card Design:** Large featured card with image, video embed, and full story **Visual Elements:** - Thumbnail image from NY Post article (if available) or stock image of recovery - NY Post logo - "Featured Story" badge - Video play button overlay **Card Content:** **Headline:** "NYC Teen Paralyzed in Fiery Car Crash Makes 'Miracle' Recovery" **Publication & Date:** **NY Post** | December 19, 2023 **Summary (2-3 sentences):** A Staten Island teenager left paralyzed and unable to speak after a devastating car accident has made a remarkable recovery that even his doctors called miraculous. Anthony Mercado, 19, defied expectations after sustaining life-threatening injuries that included brain trauma, paralysis, and inability to speak. **Dr. Rasouli's Quote (Pull Quote - Large, Styled):** > "The injuries he sustained were definitely life-threatening. He had bruises on the brain, bleeding, and a significant blood clot pressing on his brain. I admittedly had doubts about a positive outcome due to his devastating injuries." > > **— Dr. Jonathan Rasouli, Neurosurgeon** **Full Story Section (Expandable or Separate Tab):** **"I'm here today" - Anthony Mercado's Journey from Tragedy to Triumph** In June 2022, 17-year-old Anthony Mercado's life changed in an instant. Returning from a senior class trip to Seaside Heights, New Jersey, his 2008 Cadillac CTS crashed on Staten Island's South Avenue, bursting into flames with the teenager trapped inside. "I learned that cars are thousands of pounds of a weapon that can kill you at any moment," Mercado reflected on the incident that would test every fiber of his being. **A Hero's Intervention** Fortune arrived in the form of an ex-firefighter who had previously responded to 9/11. Armed with scissors, the good Samaritan cut Mercado's seatbelt and pulled him from the burning wreckage. "He thought [the devastation] was something out of a movie," Mercado recalled. **Life-Threatening Injuries** Rushed to Staten Island University Hospital in critical condition, Mercado required immediate lifesaving interventions including a breathing tube and was placed in a medically-induced coma. Dr. Jonathan Rasouli, the neurosurgeon who would guide much of his recovery, faced a daunting challenge. "He had bruises on the brain, there was bleeding, he had a significant blood clot that was pressing on his brain," Dr. Rasouli explained. "The injuries were definitely life-threatening, with pretty significant trauma to his head." **The Long Road Back** What followed were years of surgeries and rehabilitation. But Mercado refused to surrender to his circumstances. With determination fueled by family support, he began the painstaking process of recovery. His first victory came in the form of simple sounds – just "eh" – but it was progress. His family, particularly his mother and sister, maintained vigil, providing the emotional foundation for his recovery. "I could see that his family had such a fight, such a drive to see him pull through this," Dr. Rasouli noted. "I knew if he had that familial support that he'd be just fine." **"Mom" - The Word That Changed Everything** In August 2022, a breakthrough arrived. Mercado spoke his first word: "Mom." "I called for my mom, I said the word 'mom,'" he recalled emotionally. "She started to cry. Little by little, I started saying more and more words and could speak sentences by the end of that August." **Learning to Walk Again** Walking presented another mountain to climb, but Mercado and his family tackled it together. "We didn't use a wheelchair," Mercado explained. "I walked around with my right arm over my mom's shoulder. She carried me and I walked little by little over and over and over until I got to walk by myself." **A Miracle Acknowledged** Today, at 19, Anthony Mercado stands as living proof that determination, expert medical care, and family support can overcome seemingly insurmountable odds. "God brought me out of that. I'm here today," Mercado told The Post. Dr. Rasouli, who witnessed this transformation firsthand, reflected on the outcome: "He ultimately did fantastic." **Key Takeaways:** - ✓ Survived life-threatening brain trauma and paralysis - ✓ Regained ability to speak starting with single word "Mom" - ✓ Learned to walk again through family-supported rehabilitation - ✓ Multiple complex neurosurgeries performed by Dr. Rasouli - ✓ Recovery aided by strong family support system - ✓ Now living independently at age 19 [Orange Button] Read Full NY Post Article [Blue Button] Watch Recovery Video **Embedded Video Section:** ```html

Watch Anthony's Recovery Journey

Anthony Mercado shares his remarkable recovery journey after devastating car accident. Video courtesy of NY Post.

``` **Related Coverage:** - 📰 [Link] Full NY Post Article: "NYC teen paralyzed, brain damaged in fiery car crash reveals first word" - 🎥 [Link] NY Post Video Feature - 📺 [Link] Local News Coverage (if available) --- #### **Additional Featured Stories Section** **Section Title:** "More Patient Success Stories" **Story Card Template (3-column grid, responsive):** **Story Card 2:** **Title:** "Paralyzed NY Man Can Move and Feel Again — Thanks to AI 'Miracle' Surgery" **Publication:** NY Post | [Date] **Summary:** Innovative surgical technique using AI-assisted technology helps paralyzed patient regain sensation and movement. **Dr. Rasouli's Role:** [Description of his involvement] [Button] Read Story **Story Card 3:** **Title:** [Another patient success story if available] **Publication:** [Source] **Summary:** [Brief description] [Button] Read Story **Story Card 4:** **Title:** [Another patient success story if available] **Publication:** [Source] **Summary:** [Brief description] [Button] Read Story --- ### Section 2: Media Appearances & Expert Commentary **Section Title:** "Expert Medical Commentary" **Grid Layout (2 columns on desktop):** #### **National Media Features** **CNN** **Topic:** [Specific topic Dr. Rasouli discussed] **Date:** [Date] **Summary:** Dr. Rasouli provided expert commentary on [topic] [Icon] 📺 [Link] View Coverage **The Washington Post** **Topic:** Neurosurgical innovations / Spine surgery advances **Date:** [Date] **Summary:** Featured expert on [specific medical topic] [Icon] 📰 [Link] Read Article **Becker's Spine Review** **Topic:** [Industry-specific coverage] **Date:** [Date] **Summary:** [Description of coverage] [Icon] 📰 [Link] Read Article **Northwell Health Features** **Topic:** "Softening the Memory" - Memory modulation research **Date:** April 24, 2025 **Summary:** Dr. Rasouli provided critical insight into how current therapies aim to soften, not erase, painful memories [Icon] 📰 [Link] Read Article #### **Local & Regional Coverage** **Staten Island Advance** [If available - local coverage] **NY Metro Area Health Publications** [If available] --- ### Section 3: Published Research & Articles **Section Title:** "Research & Publications" **Intro Paragraph:** "As a published researcher and former Cleveland Clinic faculty member, Dr. Rasouli actively contributes to advancing the field of neurosurgery and spine surgery through peer-reviewed research, clinical studies, and educational content." **Publication Categories (Tabbed Interface):** **Tab 1: Peer-Reviewed Journal Articles** **Featured Publications (List Format):** 1. **"Assessing the Accessibility, Engagement, and Value of the Virtual Global Spine Conference as an Educational Platform for Spine: A Three-Year Review"** - Journal: *World Neurosurgery* - Date: May 2024 - Co-authors: Hussein, A., Than, K., Gibbs, W., Rasouli, J., Dahdaleh, N.S. - [Link] View Abstract | [Link] Full Article 2. **"Editorial: Optimization of spine surgery outcomes in the pre-, peri-, and postoperative settings"** - Journal: *Frontiers in Surgery* - Date: January 2023 - Co-authors: Rasouli, J.J., Steinberger, J. - [Link] View Article 3. **"Negative Pressure Wound Therapy in Spinal Surgery"** - Journal: *Bioengineering* - Date: October 2022 - Co-authors: Gilad, R., Fiani, B., Rasouli, J., Motivala, S., White, A.J. - [Link] View Abstract 4. **"Utility of Virtual Spine Neurosurgery Education for Medical Students"** - Co-authors: Shlobin, N.A., Radwanski, R.E., Kortz, M.W., Rasouli, J.J., et al. - [Link] View Abstract 5. **"Anterior Cervical Discectomy and Fusion With 'Kissing' Allograft Interbodies"** - Co-authors: Rasouli, J.J., Fiani, B., Belding, J., Moore, T.A. - [Link] View Article 6. **"Cervical Spine Alignment in the Sagittal Axis: A Review of the Best Validated Measures in Clinical Practice"** - Focus: Clinical assessment methods for cervical spine alignment - [Link] View Article 7. **"Emerging Blockchain Technology Solutions for Modern Healthcare Infrastructure"** - Journal: *Journal of Scientific Innovation in Medicine* - Date: January 2019 - Co-authors: Yaeger, K.A., Martini, M., Rasouli, J.J., Costa, A.B. - [Link] View Article 8. **"Value Analysis in Minimally Invasive Spine Surgery"** - Journal: *Contemporary Spine Surgery* - Date: April 2017 - Co-authors: Rasouli, J.J., Skovrlj, B., Qureshi, S.A. - [Link] View Article [Button] View Complete Publication List (Links to CV or separate publications page) **Tab 2: Book Chapters** **Medical Textbook Contributions:** - [List book chapters if available] - [Chapter title, book name, year, publisher] **Tab 3: Conference Presentations** **Selected Presentations:** - National neurosurgical conferences - International spine surgery symposiums - Regional medical education programs - Grand rounds presentations [Note: Obtain specific presentation titles and dates from Dr. Rasouli] --- ### Section 4: Speaking Engagements & Lectures **Section Title:** "Educational Leadership" **Content:** **Academic Teaching** - Former Assistant Professor of Neurosurgery, Cleveland Clinic Lerner College of Medicine - Guest lecturer at medical schools and residency programs - Grand rounds presentations at major medical centers **Professional Conferences** - Speaker at national neurosurgical conferences - Presenter at international spine surgery symposiums - Panelist at medical technology forums **Community Education** - Health screenings and awareness events - Community seminars on spine health - Patient education workshops [If available: Upcoming speaking engagements with dates] --- ### Section 5: Awards & Recognition **Section Title:** "Professional Recognition" **Visual: Badge/Icon Grid** **🏆 Alpha Omega Alpha Medical Honor Society** Top 10% of medical school graduating class **🏆 Board Certification** American Board of Neurological Surgery **🏆 Cleveland Clinic Faculty** Assistant Professor of Neurosurgery **🏆 Dual Fellowship Training** Neurosurgery + Orthopaedic Spine Surgery (rare distinction) **🏆 Published Researcher** 15+ peer-reviewed publications **🏆 Media-Recognized Expert** Featured in CNN, Washington Post, NY Post, Becker's Spine **🏆 Perfect Patient Rating** 5.0 stars across all review platforms **🏆 Hospital Affiliations** Northwell Health - Staten Island University Hospital, Lenox Hill Hospital [Additional awards/recognition as available from Dr. Rasouli] --- ### Section 6: Video Gallery **Section Title:** "Watch Dr. Rasouli's Expertise in Action" **Video Grid (2-3 columns, responsive):** **Video 1: Patient Success Story - Anthony Mercado** [Embedded video from NY Post] **Title:** "Teen's Miracle Recovery After Devastating Accident" **Duration:** [X:XX] **Description:** Anthony Mercado shares his remarkable journey from paralysis to walking again [Play Button Overlay] **Video 2: Educational Content** [If available - procedure explanations, patient education] **Title:** "Understanding Minimally Invasive Spine Surgery" **Duration:** [X:XX] **Description:** Dr. Rasouli explains how minimally invasive techniques benefit patients [Play Button Overlay] **Video 3: Media Interview** [If available - TV appearances, interviews] **Title:** "Expert Insights on [Topic]" **Duration:** [X:XX] **Description:** [Description] [Play Button Overlay] **Video 4: Office/Consultation** [If available - virtual office tour or consultation overview] **Title:** "Meet Dr. Rasouli" **Duration:** [X:XX] **Description:** Introduction to Dr. Rasouli's practice and approach [Play Button Overlay] --- ### Section 7: Press Kit & Media Inquiries **Section Title:** "Media Resources" **Content (2-column layout):** **For Journalists & Media:** **Press Kit Downloads:** - 📥 High-resolution headshots - 📥 Professional biography (short & long versions) - 📥 Curriculum vitae (CV) - 📥 Fact sheet (credentials, specialties, affiliations) - 📥 Logo files (practice branding if applicable) [Download Button] Download Press Kit (ZIP file) **Media Contact:** For interview requests, expert commentary, or media inquiries: **Email:** [media contact email] **Phone:** (718) 226-4940 **Response Time:** Within 24 hours **Areas of Expertise for Commentary:** - Complex spine surgery and spinal deformities - Minimally invasive surgical techniques - Robotic-assisted spine surgery - Neurosurgical innovations - Patient recovery and rehabilitation - Medical technology and AI in surgery - Spine trauma and emergency neurosurgery --- ### Section 8: Patient Stories (Brief Highlights) **Section Title:** "Lives Changed: Patient Testimonials" **Carousel/Grid of Brief Testimonials:** **Testimonial Card Template:** ``` ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "[Patient quote about their experience]" — [Patient Name + Initial] [Condition Treated] | [Month Year] [Link] Read Full Story ``` **Example Cards:** **Card 1:** ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "After my devastating accident, Dr. Rasouli gave me my life back. His expertise and dedication helped me regain abilities I thought I'd lost forever." — Anthony M. | Complex Trauma | 2022-2023 [Link] Read Anthony's Story **Card 2:** ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Dr. Rasouli's thorough review of my MRI revealed issues my previous doctor missed. His honest assessment and clear communication gave me confidence in my treatment plan." — Michael D. | Spinal Stenosis | 2024 [Link] Read Full Testimonial **Card 3:** ⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I avoided unnecessary major surgery thanks to Dr. Rasouli's second opinion. His expertise in minimally invasive options made all the difference in my recovery." — Sarah M. | Herniated Disc | 2024 [Link] Read Full Testimonial [Link] View All Patient Testimonials --- ### Section 9: Latest News & Updates **Section Title:** "Latest Updates" **Blog/News Feed Format:** **Post 1: Recent Media Feature** **Date:** [Most recent date] **Title:** [Article headline] **Source:** [Publication name] **Summary:** [2-3 sentence summary] [Button] Read More **Post 2: New Research Published** **Date:** [Date] **Title:** [Publication title] **Journal:** [Journal name] **Summary:** [Brief description of findings] [Button] View Publication **Post 3: Speaking Engagement** **Date:** [Date] **Title:** [Event name] **Location:** [City, venue] **Topic:** [Presentation topic] [Button] Learn More [Button] View All News & Updates --- ## DESIGN SPECIFICATIONS ### Color Palette - **Primary Blue:** #1E4D8B (section headers, accents) - **Secondary Blue:** #0066CC (links, buttons) - **Orange:** #FF6B35 (featured story badges, CTAs) - **White:** #FFFFFF (cards, backgrounds) - **Light Gray:** #F5F5F5 (alternating sections) - **Text:** #333333 (body text) - **Gold:** #DAA520 (awards, recognition badges) ### Typography - **Page H1:** 42px, bold, color #1E4D8B - **Section H2:** 32px, semi-bold, color #1E4D8B - **Story Headlines:** 26px, bold, color #333333 - **Body Text:** 17px, regular, color #333333, line-height 1.7 - **Pull Quotes:** 22px, italic, color #1E4D8B, with quotation marks ### Card Design **Featured Story Card (Large):** ```css .featured-story-card { background: white; border-radius: 12px; padding: 40px; box-shadow: 0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.1); margin: 40px 0; border-left: 6px solid #FF6B35; } .featured-badge { display: inline-block; background: linear-gradient(135deg, #FF6B35, #FF8C42); color: white; padding: 6px 16px; border-radius: 20px; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 700; text-transform: uppercase; margin-bottom: 16px; } .publication-info { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; margin: 16px 0; color: #666; font-size: 15px; } .pull-quote { background: #F5F8FF; border-left: 4px solid #1E4D8B; padding: 24px 32px; margin: 32px 0; font-size: 20px; font-style: italic; color: #1E4D8B; line-height: 1.6; } .pull-quote::before { content: '"'; font-size: 48px; color: #1E4D8B; opacity: 0.3; position: relative; top: 10px; } ``` **Media Appearance Card (Medium):** ```css .media-card { background: white; border-radius: 8px; padding: 24px; box-shadow: 0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.08); transition: transform 0.3s ease; } .media-card:hover { transform: translateY(-4px); box-shadow: 0 6px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.12); } .media-logo { height: 40px; margin-bottom: 16px; opacity: 0.7; filter: grayscale(100%); transition: all 0.3s ease; } .media-card:hover .media-logo { opacity: 1; filter: grayscale(0%); } ``` **Publication Card (Small):** ```css .publication-card { background: #F9F9F9; border-radius: 6px; padding: 20px; margin-bottom: 16px; border-left: 3px solid #0066CC; } .publication-title { font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; color: #333; margin-bottom: 8px; line-height: 1.4; } .publication-meta { font-size: 14px; color: #666; margin-bottom: 12px; } ``` ### Video Embed Styling **Responsive Video Container:** ```css .video-container { position: relative; width: 100%; padding-bottom: 56.25%; /* 16:9 Aspect Ratio */ height: 0; overflow: hidden; border-radius: 8px; box-shadow: 0 4px 20px rgba(0,0,0,0.15); margin: 24px 0; } .video-container iframe { position: absolute; top: 0; left: 0; width: 100%; height: 100%; border: 0; } .video-play-overlay { position: absolute; top: 50%; left: 50%; transform: translate(-50%, -50%); width: 80px; height: 80px; background: rgba(255,107,53,0.9); border-radius: 50%; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.3s ease; } .video-play-overlay:hover { background: rgba(255,107,53,1); transform: translate(-50%, -50%) scale(1.1); } .video-play-icon { width: 0; height: 0; border-left: 25px solid white; border-top: 15px solid transparent; border-bottom: 15px solid transparent; margin-left: 5px; } ``` ### Button Styles **Primary CTA (Orange):** ```css .media-cta-primary { background: linear-gradient(135deg, #FF6B35, #FF8C42); color: white; padding: 14px 32px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; display: inline-block; transition: all 0.3s ease; box-shadow: 0 4px 12px rgba(255,107,53,0.3); } .media-cta-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-2px); box-shadow: 0 6px 16px rgba(255,107,53,0.4); } ``` **Secondary CTA (Blue):** ```css .media-cta-secondary { background: white; color: #0066CC; border: 2px solid #0066CC; padding: 12px 28px; border-radius: 6px; font-size: 16px; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none; display: inline-block; transition: all 0.3s ease; } .media-cta-secondary:hover { background: #0066CC; color: white; } ``` ### Mobile Responsiveness **Breakpoints:** ```css /* Desktop: 1024px+ */ .media-grid { display: grid; grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); gap: 32px; } /* Tablet: 768px - 1023px */ @media (max-width: 1023px) { .media-grid { grid-template-columns: 1fr; gap: 24px; } .featured-story-card { padding: 24px; } } /* Mobile: 320px - 767px */ @media (max-width: 767px) { .page-h1 { font-size: 32px; } .section-h2 { font-size: 26px; } .pull-quote { padding: 20px; font-size: 18px; } .video-play-overlay { width: 60px; height: 60px; } } ``` --- ## TECHNICAL IMPLEMENTATION ### Video Embedding Options **Option 1: NY Post Video Embed (Preferred)** ```html ``` **Option 2: YouTube (If uploaded to channel)** ```html ``` **Option 3: Vimeo (If uploaded)** ```html ``` **Option 4: Self-Hosted Video** ```html ``` ### SEO Optimization **Schema Markup for Video:** ```json { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "VideoObject", "name": "NYC Teen's Miracle Recovery - Dr. Rasouli Patient Story", "description": "Anthony Mercado's remarkable recovery from paralysis after devastating car accident", "thumbnailUrl": "[THUMBNAIL_URL]", "uploadDate": "2023-12-19", "duration": "PT[X]M[Y]S", "contentUrl": "[VIDEO_URL]" } ``` **Schema Markup for News Article:** ```json { "@context": "https://schema.org", "@type": "NewsArticle", "headline": "NYC teen paralyzed, brain damaged in fiery car crash reveals first word after 'miracle' recovery", "datePublished": "2023-12-19", "author": { "@type": "Organization", "name": "NY Post" }, "publisher": { "@type": "Organization", "name": "NY Post" } } ``` ### Analytics Tracking **Events to Track:** - Video plays (start, 25%, 50%, 75%, 100%) - External link clicks (to NY Post article) - Publication PDF downloads - Media inquiry form submissions - Social shares of stories - Time spent on featured stories - Scroll depth on long-form content ### Social Sharing Integration **Share Buttons for Featured Stories:** ```html
Share this story: Facebook Twitter LinkedIn Email
``` --- ## NAVIGATION INTEGRATION ### Main Navigation Add "Media" to primary navigation menu: ``` Home | About Dr. Rasouli | Services | Patient Resources | Media | Contact ``` ### Footer Add under "About" section: ``` About Dr. Rasouli: - Biography - Credentials - Media & Press ← NEW - Publications ``` ### Homepage Integration Add featured media section on homepage: **"Dr. Rasouli in the News"** Featured in CNN, Washington Post, and NY Post [Link with image] Read Anthony's Miracle Recovery Story → --- ## CTA STRATEGY **Throughout the Page:** **Primary CTAs:** - [Orange Button] Schedule Consultation - [Orange Button] Free MRI Review - [Blue Button] Call (718) 226-4940 **Secondary CTAs:** - [Link] Read Full Article - [Link] Watch Video - [Link] View All Publications - [Link] Download Press Kit - [Link] Contact for Media Inquiries **Bottom of Page CTA:** ``` Inspired by These Stories? If you or a loved one is facing a complex spine condition, Dr. Rasouli is here to help. [Orange Button] Schedule Your Consultation [Blue Button] Request Free MRI Review Available in Staten Island & Manhattan (718) 226-4940 ``` --- ## CONTENT MAINTENANCE PLAN **Regular Updates:** - **Monthly:** Add new media appearances as they occur - **Quarterly:** Update publication list with new research - **Annually:** Refresh patient testimonials (with consent) - **As Needed:** Add new patient success stories **Content Sources:** - Google Alerts for "Dr. Jonathan Rasouli" - PubMed alerts for new publications - Media monitoring for features - Patient testimonial requests - Social media mentions --- ## LEGAL & COMPLIANCE **Patient Story Consent:** - ✅ Written permission from Anthony Mercado (or his family) - ✅ HIPAA release for using medical information - ✅ Media release for photos/videos - ✅ Attribution to original NY Post article **Copyright Considerations:** - ✅ Link to original NY Post article (not reproduce entire text) - ✅ Properly embed NY Post video (not download/re-upload) - ✅ Use quotations with attribution - ✅ Credit all media sources properly **Disclaimers:** "Patient outcomes vary. The results described are specific to individual patients and should not be considered typical or guaranteed. Past performance does not guarantee future results." --- ## SUCCESS METRICS **KPIs to Track:** - Page views on Media page - Video play rate and completion rate - External link clicks to original articles - Time on page (target: 3+ minutes) - Social shares of patient stories - Media inquiry form submissions - Impact on overall site credibility/trust - Increase in consultation bookings after visiting Media page --- ## IMPLEMENTATION CHECKLIST **Before Launch:** - [ ] Obtain patient consent (Anthony Mercado) - [ ] Get high-res images from NY Post (or use with attribution) - [ ] Embed or link to video properly - [ ] Verify all publication citations accurate - [ ] Test video playback on all devices - [ ] Ensure mobile responsiveness - [ ] Add schema markup for SEO - [ ] Set up analytics tracking - [ ] Review legal compliance - [ ] Proofread all content **Post-Launch:** - [ ] Monitor video engagement - [ ] Track page performance - [ ] Gather additional patient stories - [ ] Update with new media appearances - [ ] Share page on social media - [ ] Send to media contacts for awareness --- ## SUMMARY This Media & Press page serves multiple strategic purposes: 1. **Credibility Building** - Showcases Dr. Rasouli as trusted media expert 2. **Patient Inspiration** - Demonstrates real results through powerful stories 3. **Thought Leadership** - Highlights research and academic contributions 4. **Trust Signals** - Media logos and patient success stories build confidence 5. **Engagement Tool** - Video content keeps visitors on site longer 6. **SEO Benefits** - Rich media content improves search rankings 7. **Media Relations** - Provides resources for journalists **Priority:** HIGH - This page significantly enhances Dr. Rasouli's authority, differentiates his practice, and provides compelling social proof that converts visitors into patients. The Anthony Mercado story is particularly powerful as it demonstrates not just surgical skill but compassion, dedication to difficult cases, and life-changing outcomes - exactly what prospective patients want to see in their neurosurgeon.

**Building The New Media Page**

I've initiated the creation of the new "Media & Press" page for Dr. Rasouli's website. Right now, I'm working on the `pages/Media.tsx` component, based on the provided specifications. I'll need to update `constants.tsx` to integrate the correct route.


**Defining Page Components**

I'm now focusing on the components of the "Media & Press" page. I've broken down the content into sections: Hero, Stats Bar, Featured Stories (with Anthony Mercado's piece needing special layout), Expert Commentary, and Research & Publications. I might need to introduce new data structures or hardcode some content for now. Next, I plan to integrate all these sections, along with updating app routing and layout components.


**Outlining Media Content**

I'm currently structuring the various media content types. I'm planning to use a grid for awards and video galleries, along with a carousel or grid for patient stories. I'm leaning toward a blog-like feed for the latest news. I'll need to remember to integrate responsive video containers. The next step is modifying `pages/Home.tsx` to incorporate the "Dr. Rasouli in the News" section. I've also updated the `App.tsx` routing. I think this will wrap it up for this stage.


**Incorporating Home Page Features**

I've just added the "Featured Media" section to the homepage in `pages/Home.tsx`, and I put it after "About", before "Testimonials", as requested. I'm also preparing to integrate the "Media" page in `constants.tsx` and `App.tsx`. I'm starting work on the code for `Media.tsx` and planning to utilize data arrays.


**Implementing The Navigation Links**

I've updated `constants.tsx` to include the "Media" item in `NAV_LINKS`, and since `Layout.tsx` already iterates through `NAV_LINKS`, this update will reflect in both the header and footer, as it doesn't get filtered. Next, I'll create the `Media.tsx` page, add the corresponding route in `App.tsx`, and integrate the "Media" section on the homepage in `pages/Home.tsx`.


**Revising Navigation Structure**

I've just been updating the site navigation. I'm adding the new "Media" section to the navigation and am placing it before "Free MRI Review" in `constants.tsx`. This should reflect in both the header and footer because `Layout.tsx` directly iterates through the array. I'm continuing to integrate the new page and am moving forward!

I will implement the new "Media & Press" page, update the navigation, and integrate a media highlight section onto the homepage.

Here are the changes:

1.  **`constants.tsx`**: Add the "Media" link to the navigation menu.

payload.text:
**Beginning a Deep Dive**

I'm currently breaking down the request: the addition of social media buttons under Dr. Rasouli's headshot and an "Featured In" carousel. I'm focusing on defining the specific components of each element to ensure complete and accurate implementation.


**Formulating the Implementation**

I've moved on to the implementation strategy. My plan involves updating `constants.tsx` with social media links and media logos for data management. Next, I'll build reusable `SocialButtons` and `MediaCarousel` components, essential for the described sections, to ensure scalability and easier updates in the future. I'm focusing on component reusability and keeping the code organized.


**Finalizing Integration Points**

I'm now fully immersed in the practical integration. I'll insert the `SocialButtons` directly under Dr. Rasouli's headshot within the `About.tsx` page, as planned. The `MediaCarousel` will be placed near the "Media & Research" area, replacing the current static grid. I'm also adding this carousel to both `pages/Home.tsx` and `pages/Media.tsx`, strategically placing it near the hero section and within the existing media content respectively. This multi-page integration assures comprehensive coverage and easy navigation of media features.


**Evaluating Style Choices**

I'm now focusing on the style choices and will leverage Tailwind's utilities where possible. For animations, I'll assess whether to use a style block or add custom styles. I've also reconsidered the image approach, deciding to use text-based SVG data URIs or placeholder services for the media logos, ensuring visual integrity without requiring actual image uploads.



**Creating Components and Files**

I'm now writing the necessary components and files. I'm focusing on the `SocialButtons` and `MediaCarousel` components. For the carousel, I'm generating text-based SVG data URIs as placeholders for the media logos, guaranteeing the user sees "CNN," "NY Post," and similar labels immediately. I will use inline styles for the animation, so no external CSS files are needed.


**Finalizing File Integrations**

I'm now integrating the components into the necessary pages. I'll add `<SocialButtons />` to `pages/About.tsx`, directly below the image. Then, I will add `<MediaCarousel />` to `pages/Home.tsx` and `pages/Media.tsx`. I am moving to finalize the "logos" issue in the carousel by using placeholders. I will be sure to use `https://placehold.co` with text to simulate these logos. Lastly, I'll review and ensure my plan meets the original request.


**Finalizing the Display**

I'm now fully immersed in the component placements. I will integrate the `SocialButtons` component directly into the `About.tsx` page, specifically in the image column. Following that, I'm integrating the `MediaCarousel` section into both the `Home.tsx` and `Media.tsx` pages, in the areas planned. I've switched my approach on the carousel logos to placeholder images with text, using `placehold.co` to generate images like `placehold.co/200x80/white/1E4D8B?text=LOGO_NAME`, to make the mockups look complete.

I will create two new components: `SocialButtons` and `MediaCarousel`, and then integrate them into the `About`, `Home`, and `Media` pages as requested.

Here are the changes:

1.  **`components/SocialButtons.tsx`**: A new component containing the specific SVG icons and styling for LinkedIn, Instagram, X, and Doximity.
2.  **`components/MediaCarousel.tsx`**: A new component implementing the infinite scrolling animation with media logos.
3.  **`pages/About.tsx`**: Adding the social buttons under the headshot and replacing the static media grid with the new carousel.
4.  **`pages/Home.tsx`**: Adding the featured carousel to the homepage.
5.  **`pages/Media.tsx`**: Adding the featured carousel to the media page.

**Developing New Social Component**

I'm now building a fresh component, `HeadshotSocials.tsx`, designed explicitly for the circular social buttons beneath the headshot on the home page. **Refactoring Button Styles**

I'm now revising my approach for the social media buttons. I've realized `HeadshotSocials.tsx` might work well to refactor the `SocialButtons` component. So, I will create `SocialLinks.tsx` or `HeadshotSocials.tsx` to accommodate the circular icon style. The current plan is to use this component on both the home and about pages. **Incorporating Icon Updates**

**Streamlining Button Component**

I'm currently focused on the credentials badges section. I've decided to include the Castle Connolly Rising Star recognitions (2023-2025), dual fellowship training at Cleveland Clinic, and board certification. I'm exploring the best way to visually present these badges for maximum impact and clarity, and I will be working on a design mock-up soon.

I'm now incorporating additional credentials into the design, specifically my ABNS certification, Alpha Omega Alpha membership, Cleveland Clinic faculty position, and research publications. I've decided on the component structure, `CredentialsBadges.tsx`, and will integrate it into both the home and about pages. I'm focusing on converting the provided HTML/CSS into a React component using Tailwind CSS for styling.


**Designing Visual Components**

I'm now focusing on implementing the visual design for the credentials badges. I plan to use Tailwind utility classes for styling and have defined the color palette. For the badge icons, I'll prioritize `lucide-react` for consistency, but will use the provided SVGs for any custom designs in the provided prompt, ensuring visual fidelity.


**Visualizing Badges' Structure**

I'm now focusing on integrating the badge SVGs directly into React components to maintain visual integrity. I'm prioritizing inline components for fidelity. I've analyzed the SVGs provided, which include a star, shield, check, medal, building, and document shape. I'm working to place the `<CredentialsBadges />` component in both `Home.tsx` and `About.tsx` after hero or trust elements. I'll place the badges section below the hero section on the homepage and will consider if the new section should replace or augment the Trust Bar.


**Implementing Credential Placement**

I'm now determining the most effective placement of the credentials section. On the homepage, it will go between the Hero/TrustBar and MediaCarousel/Services. On the about page, I'll place it after the Biography section. I've started planning `CredentialsBadges.tsx`, focusing on section structure and styling elements.


**Constructing the Components**

I'm now fully immersed in the implementation phase. I'm focusing on the component's structure within `CredentialsBadges.tsx`, including styling and the specified top borders, icons, and hover effects. I'm actively copying the SVG paths provided to ensure fidelity, and double-checked that the correct fonts (Poppins and Open Sans) are loaded. I have decided on the content and link for Badge 1: Castle Connolly Rising Star (2023-2025). The updated files will be `CredentialsBadges.tsx`, `Home.tsx` and `About.tsx`.


**Finalizing Visual Presentation**

I'm now integrating this component into the project. I'm focusing on ensuring it looks great. The current task is adding the new `<CredentialsBadges />` component with the provided HTML/CSS, and adapting it to fit the existing visual style. I've double-checked my work to ensure that all the badges are in the correct place, using inline styling for the SVG components. I'm also reviewing how the section looks with the hero and trust elements.


**Reviewing Visual Fidelity**

I'm presently focusing on how the credentials section looks, reviewing the visual fidelity. I've been integrating the provided HTML/CSS into the component, and am trying to match the existing visual style of the website. I have double-checked that the `CredentialsBadges` component is in place, using inline styling for the SVG components. I'm also ensuring the section complements the hero and trust elements.


**Fine-tuning Aesthetics**

I'm currently focused on the visual presentation of the badges. I've been integrating specific details, such as the `Castle Connolly Rising Star` badge, and I'm ensuring all badges are consistent with the existing theme. I'm focusing on the hover effects and transitions. My key concern is balancing visual appeal with clarity.


**Implementing New Badge Details**

I'm now incorporating specific details for the Cleveland Clinic Dual Fellowship badge, ensuring the provided HTML/CSS is seamlessly integrated. I'm focusing on hover effects and transitions for enhanced visual appeal and usability. I'm balancing visual appeal with clarity, ensuring the badge accurately reflects the training details. I'm focusing on the component's structure in `CredentialsBadges.tsx`.


**Defining Visual Style**

I'm now working to style the "Board Certified" badge using the provided HTML/CSS. I'm focusing on Tailwind classes for the text and hover effects. I'll maintain the existing theme, including colors and fonts. The component structure will be `CredentialsBadges.tsx`.


**Incorporating New Icons**

I'm presently integrating the "Alpha Omega Alpha" badge and icon. I'm focusing on incorporating the provided SVG path directly into the `CredentialsBadges.tsx` component, ensuring it fits with the existing design language. I've chosen to use inline styling to maintain the visual fidelity of the SVG. I'm focusing on ensuring it looks consistent with other badges' styling.


**Implementing Last Badge**

I'm now integrating the final badge, "Top 10% Graduate", into the `CredentialsBadges.tsx` component. I'm focused on matching the style, including text, colors, and the hover effect. The component includes a custom SVG and will be inline styled. I am aiming for visual consistency with the other badges.


**Updating Published Credentials**

I'm now integrating the "Academic Faculty" badge from Cleveland Clinic Lerner College of Medicine, ensuring the visual style is maintained. I'm prioritizing direct SVG path integration for the icon. I am working on the badge's layout and content, including the name, faculty position, and a brief description. I'll test hover effects and transitions for visual appeal. I'm focusing on the component's structure in `CredentialsBadges.tsx`.


**Implementing Publication Badge**

I'm now integrating the "Published Researcher" badge with its SVG icon into the `CredentialsBadges.tsx` component. I'm prioritizing direct SVG path integration and ensuring visual consistency with existing badges' styling. I'm focusing on text styling and hover effects, as well as the new placement and formatting within the section. I have updated the `Home.tsx` file to display the badge.


**Revising Homepage Placement**

I've just been reviewing the homepage structure again. I've decided to keep the Trust Bar as an essential summary beneath the hero section. Thus, I'll insert the new `<CredentialsBadges />` component *after* the Trust Bar and *before* the Services section, staying consistent with the original prompt. I've updated the `Home.tsx` layout to reflect this: Hero -> Trust Bar -> Services -> About -> Media -> Testimonials -> CTA. The updated layout will keep the suggested structure, `Hero -> Credentials -> Featured Media -> Services`. There is no cost and no obligation to become a patient." }, { question: "How long does the review take?", answer: "Most MRI reviews are completed within 24-48 hours. You'll receive an email when your report is ready. Complex cases may take slightly longer." }, { question: "What if I don't have digital copies of my MRI?", answer: "No problem. You can request your MRI images from the facility where you had the scan. Most imaging centers provide images on CD or can send them electronically. **Confirming File Accuracy**

I will extend the `NavItem` interface to support sub-items via a `children` array. In `constants.tsx`, I'll transform "About Dr. Rasouli" into a parent item with its dropdown children. I'm setting paths for each sub-item.


**Refining the Routing**

I'm now nailing down the routing strategy for the dropdown items. My current plan is to link "Statement" to `/about`, "Credentials/CV" to `/about#credentials`, and "Global Humanitarian Work" to `/media#global-work`. The "Blog" item will go to `/blog`, though that page is still a placeholder. I'm focusing on minimizing code changes initially, creating specific routes is a better choice.


**Adjusting the Approach**

Okay, I've revised the routing again to better match common "About" sections. The parent path `/about` will host "Statement" and "Credentials/CV," each scrolling to respective sections. "Global Humanitarian Work" will link to a designated section in `/media`. I'm now exploring how to best handle the new dropdown logic in `components/Layout.tsx`.

\n\n

**Implementing the Dropdown**

I'm focused on the UI, and I'm updating `components/Layout.tsx` now to handle the dropdown behavior. I need to make the desktop navigation render with the new design. It seems using React state with `onMouseEnter` and `onMouseLeave` will likely provide a smoother user experience, rather than relying solely on CSS hover effects. I'm focusing on responsiveness.


**Expanding the Structure**

Now, I'm adapting the mobile navigation to fit the dropdown too. I'm considering an accordion-style interaction or perhaps indented links for a clear nested display. I'm focusing on responsiveness to match the desktop behavior. The implementation details are as follows: I will add `children?: NavItem[]` to `NavItem`. I'm changing `NAV_LINKS` to include children.


**Finalizing the Design**

I'm now incorporating the dropdown logic into both desktop and mobile navigation. If an item has children, it's rendered as a parent button with a chevron icon, and the dropdown appears on hover or click. On mobile, I'm using an expandable section for the child links. I'm focusing on aesthetics. I'm making sure that the dropdown looks great, especially on mobile. I'm also addressing hash scrolling for anchor links to make sure that the experience is smooth.


**Completing the Integration**

I'm now integrating the dropdown menu across the site. I'm focusing on the responsive UI and adding the logic to toggle dropdown visibility on both desktop and mobile. I'm leveraging React's state management with `onMouseEnter` and `onMouseLeave` on the desktop, and a click-based system on mobile. I've updated both the `Layout.tsx` and `types.ts` with the changes.


**Refining the Links**

I'm now refining the links to fit the current content structure, and I'm updating the routes accordingly. The "Statement" and "Credentials/CV" are going to be on the `/about` page. "Global Humanitarian Work" will point to `/media#global-work`. I'm using an id so that it scrolls to the correct section. I'm focusing on consistency.


**Optimizing the UI**

I'm now zeroing in on UI details and refactoring dropdown rendering. For mobile, I'll use a state object to manage expanded menus. Desktop dropdowns, for simplicity and Tailwind's familiarity, will now leverage `group-hover` for display control. Accessibility considerations for focus will be a follow-up task. I'm focusing on aesthetic and usability constraints, namely shadows, rounded corners, and clear hover states.

I will update the navigation structure to include a dropdown menu for "About Dr. Rasouli" with the specified sub-items ("Statement", "Credentials/CV", "Global Humanitarian Work", "Blog").

This involves updating the type definitions, the constants file to define the hierarchy, the layout component to render the dropdowns (desktop and mobile), and adding ID anchors to the relevant pages so the links jump to the correct sections.
